# Solution

## (a) Implementation of `Canonicalize`

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width < PAD then PAD else x.width)
}
```

This function:
- Preserves the `kind` and `value` (never changes which entity is referred to)
- Sets the width to `PAD` (4) if the current width is less than 4
- Leaves the width unchanged if it's already 4 or more

## (b) `ensures` clauses for `Spec`

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); Wellformed(c)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.kind == x.kind
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.value == x.value
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); c.width >= PAD
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); x.width < PAD ==> c.width == PAD
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); Canonicalize(c) == c
{ }
```

**Explanation of each clause:**

1. **`Wellformed(c)`** — The canonicalized id is wellformed (width can hold the value)
2. **`c.kind == x.kind`** — Kind is never changed
3. **`c.value == x.value`** — Numeric value is never changed
4. **`c.width >= PAD`** — The canonical width is always at least 4
5. **`x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width`** — If already canonical, width is unchanged
6. **`x.width < PAD ==> c.width == PAD`** — If not canonical, width becomes exactly PAD
7. **`Canonicalize(c) == c`** — Idempotence: canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op
